title: aegilops caudata subsp. polyathera
intro: Aegilops caudata subsp. polyathera, also known as Persian goatgrass, is a subspecies of the Aegilops caudata plant, a type of wild grass native to the Middle East and Asia. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate conditions such as arthritis and other inflammatory diseases. The health benefits of Persian goatgrass include its ability to lower cholesterol levels, improve cardiovascular health, and even exhibit anti-cancer properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tea or tincture, or dried and used as an infusion to harness its medicinal properties.
